elements.text: Synthesis and electro-optical properties of conjugated ionic polymers
Tai-Long Gui², Sung-Ho Jinb, Jong-Wook Park°, Kwon-Taek Limd, Sang-Youl Kim, Yeong-Soon Galf*

Anewionic conjugatepolyerpoly(tyN-ethypridiniumidide)(PEMP),wssythesiz thediretpolyerizatiof tytif ethynyl-N-methylpyridiniumtetraphenylborate)(PEMPTPB)wasalsopreparedbytheion-exchangereactionofpoly(2-ethynyN- thyriinuiplhauent anions in this conjugated polymer backbone.
Keywords: Polyacetylene; 2-Ethynylpyridine; Ionic polymer; Ion-exchange reaction; Luminescence
In this paper, we report the synthesis of novel ionic conjugated polymers having different counter-anion (iodide, tetraphenylborate) and the characterization of resulting ionic conjugated polymers.

Polyacetylene and its homologues have been studied as organic semiconductors [1–3],membranes for gas separa- tion and for liquid-mixture separation [4], chiro-optical materials [5,6], side-chain liquid crystals [7–9], materials for chemical sensors [10], and materials for nonlinear optical property [11,12] and photoluminescence and elec- troluminescence properties [13–17].

The conjugated ionic polymer, poly(2-ethynyl-N-meth- ylpyridinium iodide)(PEMPI) was prepared by the direct polymerization of 2-ethynylpyridine with methyl iodide without any initiator system in methanol solvent. The polymerization was performed at 70 C after standing for 1 h because of the low boiling temperature of methy1 iodide. Another ionic polymer, poly(2-ethynyl-N-methyl- pyridinium tetraphenylborate) (PEMPTPB) was easily pre- pared by the ion-exchange reaction of PEMPI with sodium tetraphenylborate in methanol solvent. FT-IR spectra were obtained with a Bruker EQUINOX 55 spectrometer using a KBr pellet. NMR spectra were recorded on a Varian 500 MHz FT-NMR spectrometer (Model: Unity INOVA) in DMSO-d6. X-ray diffractograms were obtained with a Phillips X-ray diffractometer (Model: XPert-APD). The

Polyacetylenes with ionic nature have potential as mate- rials for mixed ionic and electronic conductivities, energy storage devices such as batteries and permselective mem- brane, and light-emitting devices due to the their extensive conjugation and various functionalities [2,18]. The synthesis of simple mono- and disubstituted ionic polyacetylenes from ethynylpyridines had been carried out with the activa- tion of the acetylene bond in ethynylpyridines [18,19]. We have also studied on the synthesis and characterization of the poly(2-ethynylpyridine)-based conjugated polymers having various functionalities [2,9,14].
